name: Rudy Vallée
abstract: Hubert Prior Vallée (July 28, 1901 – July 3, 1986), known professionally as Rudy Vallée, was an American singer, musician, actor, and radio host. He was one of the first modern pop stars of the teen idol type.

works.t: Porgy and Bess: Act II, Scene 1. I Got Plenty o' Nuttin'

about_quotes.speaker: Peter Ecklund
about_quotes.evidence: he had played with Rudy Vallee’s orchestra back in the 1920s so he knew a lot of those old songs and like that.
about_quotes.doc_title: Peter Ecklund interviewed by Monk Rowe, San Diego, California, February 14, 1998

about_quotes.speaker: George Wein
about_quotes.evidence: He came out of Island Pond, Vermont, the hometown of Rudy Vallee.
about_quotes.doc_title: George Wein — Smithsonian Jazz Oral History

about_quotes.speaker: Walter P. Thomas
about_quotes.evidence: He picked that jazz up from Rudy Vallee. I saw when he made the change. We used to do broadcasts or something, and Rudy Vallee one day... There was a place where Rudy Vallee was rehearsing his band.

about_quotes.speaker: Red Norvo
about_quotes.evidence: Rudy Vallee to make his appearance at Bar Harbor in the same ballroom... Walter Gross and Mickey Bloom were standing there and they were telling Rudy Vallee

about_quotes.speaker: Phoebe Jacobs and Jackie Harris
about_quotes.evidence: A man by the name of Rudy Vallee was an ardent fan of Louis Armstrong’s... Rudy Vallee said I want Louis Armstrong to replace me for two months.
about_quotes.doc_title: Phoebe Jacobs and Jackie Harris interviewed by Monk Rowe, Rome, New York, August 21, 2006

about_quotes.speaker: Loretta Rowland Evans
about_quotes.evidence: And Rudy Vallée was on in them days. But he didn't play jazz. He played the sweetest music this side of heaven, soft music.
